description: Replace the VitePress documentation site with Astro Starlight, styled with the exact RetroUI NeoBrutalism CSS theme. The theme uses the exact @theme block, CSS custom properties, shadow scale, and color tokens from the RetroUI Vite install guide at ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/content/docs/install/vite.mdx. All interactive effects (button press, card hover, shadow transitions) are achieved with the same Tailwind utility classes RetroUI uses -- no React components needed for the doc chrome. Starlight component overrides apply RetroUI's exact class patterns to navigation, pagination, and hero elements.

userStories.title: Scaffold Astro Starlight project in docs/ directory
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a fresh Astro Starlight project in the docs/ directory so that we have the foundation for the new documentation site
acceptanceCriteria: docs/ directory exists with a working Astro Starlight project
astro.config.mjs has basic Starlight integration configured with title 'OpenTabs'
Running 'bun run dev' from docs/ starts the dev server without errors
Running 'bun run build' from docs/ produces a static site in docs/dist/
A placeholder index page renders with the Starlight default theme
docs/ has its own tsconfig.json (NOT referenced from root tsconfig.json -- standalone like plugins/)
docs/ is NOT added to root bun workspaces
docs/ has .gitignore for node_modules/ and dist/
userStories.notes: Scaffold: bun create astro -- --template starlight (or manually). The docs/ directory must be fully standalone with its own package.json, node_modules, bun.lock -- exactly like plugins/ directories. Do NOT add docs/ to root tsconfig.json references or bun workspaces.

userStories.title: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 with exact RetroUI theme
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Tailwind CSS v4 with the exact RetroUI NeoBrutalism theme so that all styling matches RetroUI precisely
acceptanceCriteria: tw-animate-css is installed (required for Tooltip/Accordion animations)

global.css contains a @theme block copied EXACTLY from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/content/docs/install/vite.mdx step 7.2, with literal font names: --font-head: 'Archivo Black', sans-serif; --font-sans: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif;
The @theme block contains the exact shadow scale: --shadow-xs through --shadow-2xl using hard-offset values with var(--border)

The @theme block contains all --color-* token mappings (background, foreground, primary, primary-foreground, secondary, secondary-foreground, primary-hover, card, card-foreground, muted, muted-foreground, accent, accent-foreground, destructive, destructive-foreground, border)

:root block has EXACT values from vite.mdx: --radius: 0, --background: #fff, --foreground: #000, --card: #fff, --card-foreground: #000, --primary: #ffdb33, --primary-hover: #ffcc00, --primary-foreground: #000, --secondary: #000, --secondary-foreground: #fff, --muted: #aeaeae, --muted-foreground: #5a5a5a, --accent: #fae583, --accent-foreground: #000, --destructive: #e63946, --destructive-foreground: #fff, --border: #000

.dark block has EXACT values from vite.mdx: --radius: 0, --background: #1a1a1a, --foreground: #f5f5f5, --card: #242424, --card-foreground: #f5f5f5, --primary: #ffdb33, --primary-hover: #ffcc00, --primary-foreground: #000, --secondary: #3a3a3a, --secondary-foreground: #f5f5f5, --muted: #3f3f46, --muted-foreground: #a0a0a0, --accent: #fae583, --accent-foreground: #000, --destructive: #e63946, --destructive-foreground: #fff, --border: #3a3a3a
Google Fonts loaded via @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Archivo+Black&family=Space+Grotesk:wght@300..700&display=swap') or via Starlight head config
Starlight --sl-* variables overridden to map to RetroUI tokens
astro.config.mjs includes global.css in Starlight's customCss array
Running 'bun run build' from docs/ succeeds
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: The @theme, :root, and .dark blocks MUST be copied exactly from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/content/docs/install/vite.mdx (step 7 'Add Theme', the code block between lines 141-211 of the mdx file). Do NOT invent values. For Starlight integration, follow https://starlight.astro.build/guides/css-and-tailwind/ but adapt for Tailwind v4. Override Starlight variables to align with RetroUI: --sl-color-accent-high: var(--primary); --sl-color-hairline: var(--border); --sl-color-text: var(--foreground); --sl-color-bg: var(--background); etc. For font loading, the Vite install guide (vite.mdx lines 120-128) shows the exact <link> tags for Google Fonts.

userStories.title: Migrate all markdown content from VitePress to Starlight
userStories.description: As a user, I want all existing documentation content available in the new Starlight site
acceptanceCriteria: All 14 markdown files from website/ are migrated to docs/src/content/docs/
Guide section: what-is-opentabs.md, getting-started.md, how-it-works.md, install-extension.md, mcp-server.md, connect-claude.md, install-plugin.md, plugin-config.md
Each file has correct Starlight frontmatter (title, description)
Internal links updated from VitePress to Starlight paths
Code blocks, tables, and ASCII diagrams render correctly
bun run build from docs/ succeeds
userStories.notes:
Starlight content: src/content/docs/guide/*.md, src/content/docs/plugins/*.md, src/content/docs/reference/*.md. Frontmatter: ---
title: 'Page Title'
description: 'Brief desc'
---. VitePress index.md hero section is handled separately in US-006.

userStories.title: Configure sidebar navigation matching VitePress structure
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar to match the existing documentation structure
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar has Guide section with groups: Introduction, Setup, Using Plugins
Sidebar has Plugins section: Overview, Creating a Plugin, Plugin SDK
Sidebar has Reference section: Configuration, CLI
Edit link configured for the GitHub repo
bun run build from docs/ succeeds
userStories.notes: Configure in astro.config.mjs starlight({ sidebar: [...] }). Social: social: [{ icon: 'github', label: 'GitHub', href: 'https://github.com/opentabs-dev/opentabs' }]. Edit link: editLink: { baseUrl: 'https://github.com/opentabs-dev/opentabs/edit/main/docs/' }.

userStories.title: Override Starlight components with RetroUI NeoBrutalism styling and interactions
userStories.description: As a user, I want navigation and structural components styled with RetroUI NeoBrutalism including all hover/press animations
acceptanceCriteria: Header has border-b-2 using var(--border) and hard offset shadow

Pagination Prev/Next buttons have the EXACT RetroUI Button default variant classes: 'shadow-md hover:shadow active:shadow-none bg-primary text-primary-foreground border-2 border-black transition hover:translate-y-1 active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1 hover:bg-primary-hover'
Pagination buttons have the press-down effect on click (active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1)
Sidebar active item has border-l-2 using var(--primary) color
Links in content have hover:text-primary transition
Component overrides registered in astro.config.mjs components: {}
All overrides preserve default Starlight functionality (navigation, search, theme toggle)
bun run build from docs/ succeeds
userStories.notes: Use Starlight wrap-and-extend pattern for Header, Pagination, Footer. The RetroUI button interactive effects are PURE CSS -- they work on any element with the right Tailwind classes. For the Pagination override, apply the exact classes from RetroUI Button.tsx default variant to the <a> links: 'font-head transition-all rounded outline-hidden cursor-pointer duration-200 font-medium flex justify-center items-center shadow-md hover:shadow active:shadow-none bg-primary text-primary-foreground border-2 border-black transition hover:translate-y-1 active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1 hover:bg-primary-hover px-4 py-1.5 text-base'. Note: the button uses 'border-black' not 'border-border' -- this is intentional in RetroUI source. With --radius: 0, 'rounded' = border-radius: 0 (sharp corners). For secondary/outline buttons (e.g. Prev button), use the outline variant: 'shadow-md hover:shadow active:shadow-none bg-transparent border-2 transition hover:translate-y-1 active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1'.

userStories.title: Create RetroUI-styled hero landing page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the landing page to have a bold NeoBrutalism hero section
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page (src/content/docs/index.mdx) uses template: splash in frontmatter
Hero displays: OpenTabs name in font-head (Archivo Black), tagline, two CTA buttons
Primary button (Get Started) has exact RetroUI Button default variant classes
Secondary button (GitHub) has exact RetroUI Button outline variant classes
Both buttons have the press-down hover/active effects (translate-y, translate-x, shadow transitions)
Feature cards below hero have exact RetroUI Card classes: 'border-2 rounded shadow-md transition-all hover:shadow-none bg-card'
6 features from original site: Plugin Architecture, Your Session Your Access, MCP Native, Hot Reload, Build Your Own, Chrome Extension
Hero uses RetroUI primary color #ffdb33 for accent elements
bun run build from docs/ succeeds
userStories.notes: The hero can use Starlight's hero frontmatter or a custom component. Feature cards use Tailwind utility classes matching RetroUI Card.tsx: 'border-2 rounded shadow-md transition-all hover:shadow-none bg-card'. With --radius: 0, 'rounded' produces sharp corners. The RetroUI Card also has 'inline-block' but for a card grid use 'block' instead via className override. Card hover effect (shadow disappears) is pure CSS -- works without React.

userStories.title: Style markdown content elements with RetroUI NeoBrutalism
userStories.description: As a user, I want code blocks, tables, and callouts to match the RetroUI aesthetic
acceptanceCriteria: Code blocks have border-2 using var(--border) and shadow from RetroUI shadow scale (e.g. shadow-md = 4px 4px 0 0 var(--border))
Tables have border-2 using var(--border) on wrapper and cells
Blockquotes have thick left border with var(--primary) color
Inline code has bg-muted with slight border
Starlight aside/callout components have border-2 and RetroUI hard shadows
All shadows are hard-offset (no blur) matching RetroUI shadow scale
bun run build from docs/ succeeds
userStories.notes: Add CSS rules in global.css targeting .sl-markdown-content elements. Key selectors: .sl-markdown-content pre, .sl-markdown-content table, .sl-markdown-content blockquote, .sl-markdown-content :is(h1,h2,h3,h4). Apply: border: 2px solid var(--border); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md). Headings: font-family: var(--font-head). The RetroUI shadow-md is '4px 4px 0 0 var(--border)'. Dark mode: --border changes to #3a3a3a automatically via the .dark CSS vars.

userStories.title: Remove old VitePress website/ directory and update root configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the old VitePress site removed
acceptanceCriteria: Root tsconfig.json, package.json, eslint, knip configs do not reference website/
No broken imports or references to website/ in the codebase
.gitignore has entries for docs/node_modules and docs/dist
userStories.notes: rm -rf website/. Check root configs for references. docs/ stays standalone, NOT added to root build tooling.
